new_classes.label_en: Other wine (neither PDO/PGI nor Tokaj, non-semi-sparkling) in containers > 2 L (subheading 2204 29 90)
new_classes.label_de: Anderer Wein (weder g.U./g.g.A. noch Tokaj, nicht perlend) in Behältnissen > 2 L (Unterposition 2204 29 90)
new_classes.definition_en: Still wine of fresh grapes in containers holding more than 2 litres, without a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO), Protected Geographical Indication (PGI), or Tokaj designation, and not being semi-sparkling wine; classified under CN subheading 2204 29 90. Total dry matter content (non-volatile constituents such as sugars, glycerol, tannins, tartaric acid, colouring matter and salts) is a key classification criterion per Additional Notes 4 and 5 to Chapter 22.

coverage_explanation: The main distinguishing criteria captured are: (1) wine in containers > 2 L (via LargeContainer restriction), (2) absence of PDO/PGI quality, (3) absence of Tokaj PDO quality, and (4) total dry matter content / non-volatile dry extract constituents as a classification criterion per Additional Notes 4 and 5 to Chapter 22. The note also excludes semi-sparkling wine from this subheading (semi-sparkling is covered by 2204 29 10), but a complement restriction on SemiSparklingPressureQuality was not added as an OWL complement restriction because the complement pattern via this tool is intended for class-level complements rather than quality-negation patterns; this gap slightly reduces coverage. Additionally, the precise numeric thresholds for total dry matter content referenced in Additional Note 4A (e.g. g/l breakpoints distinguishing dry, medium-dry, medium-sweet, sweet) are not yet reified as datatype range restrictions on TotalDryMatterContent, reducing coverage further. These thresholds would require a dedicated data property on total dry matter content and additional range restrictions to be fully axiomatised.
